Thanks for your interest, Rafael. As you saw, the quote for one year is twelve thousand dollars.
Thank you, Sofia. The proposal looks good, but the price is a little over our budget this year.
I understand. What did you have in mind?
Would you consider a discount for a two-year commitment instead of one?
For a two-year commitment, I could offer ten percent off, but I would need faster payment.
That would work. We would accept the longer term and faster payment, but only with support included.

Hi, this is Rafael with a quick update after my call with the software supplier, before our budget meeting. The good news is that we have a deal, and it is a better one than the first quote. You remember the problem: their opening price was about fifteen percent over our budget for this year, so I could not just accept it. I went into the call with a clear plan. I knew which terms mattered most to us, and I knew the one number I would be happy with. On the call I stayed polite. I thanked them for the proposal, but I was honest that the price was too high. Then I asked the key question: would they consider a discount for a longer commitment? They were flexible. They offered ten percent off if we signed for two years instead of one, but in return they wanted faster payment. So I made a counteroffer. I said we would accept the two-year term and the faster payment, but only if full support was included at no extra cost. After a few minutes, we reached an agreement that works for both sides. We get a better price and full support; they get a longer deal. I will send the new quote and the terms around before the meeting. Thanks.

Hello Rafael, this is Sofia from the vendor team, following up on our call with a revised offer in writing, as you asked. Thank you again for a friendly and professional conversation. Here is where we have landed. On the price, we are happy to give you the ten percent discount we discussed, in exchange for the two-year commitment. On payment, we would still like the faster thirty-day terms, and I can see from our notes that you are comfortable with that. And on your main request, yes, we agree: full technical support is now included at no extra cost for the whole length of the contract. So, to recap the agreement: a two-year deal, ten percent off the original quote, thirty-day payment, and support included. I think that is a fair result for both sides. I will put all of this into a formal proposal and send it over for signature this week. If anything looks different from what you expected, just give me a call and we will sort it out. Looking forward to working with your team. Thanks, Rafael.
